title: integration_smoke_select_past_note_handler_logic does NOT invoke select_past_note — iter-1 antipattern (FIND-S4-IMPL-002) recurs verbatim, only the deferral comment is new
description: iter-1 FIND-S4-IMPL-002 found that select_past_note 'integration' tests reconstruct the handler's logic chain (scan_vault_feed → parse_markdown_to_blocks → compose_state_for_select_past_note → make_editing_state_changed_payload) instead of invoking the actual `feed.rs::select_past_note` Tauri command. iter-2 introduces `integration_smoke_select_past_note_handler_logic` which the function name implies will close the gap. It does not. Lines 713, 731, 741, 747 invoke (in order): scan_vault_feed, parse_markdown_to_blocks, compose_state_for_select_past_note, make_editing_state_changed_payload — directly, in the test body. The actual `select_past_note` defined in feed.rs is never referenced. This is the EXACT antipattern from iter-1, with new lipstick: the test name now contains the word 'integration' and a doc comment now apologises for the gap with a 'Sprint 5 deferral' justification (lines 683-689). The justification cites Tauri 2's lack of emit history without the `test` feature, framing this as the only path. iter-1 FIND-S4-IMPL-002.remediation specifically proposed an alternative that does not require Tauri's `test` feature: 'introduce a mock AppHandle / Emitter trait to drive feed.rs::select_past_note end-to-end and assert on captured events (preferred), or refactor select_past_note so its observable IPC composition is in a separately testable pure function with full keyset coverage that the tests genuinely exercise'. Neither route was attempted. The test still cannot detect: (a) `select_past_note` accidentally not calling parse_markdown_to_blocks, (b) `select_past_note` calling compose with the wrong args (e.g., body instead of blocks), (c) `select_past_note` emitting events in the wrong order (EC-FEED-017), (d) `select_past_note` skipping the editor emit entirely. The deferral note acknowledges (c) and (d) but not (a) and (b), which a mock-emitter approach would catch.
remediation: Either (a) refactor `select_past_note` in feed.rs to take an `Emitter` trait object (or generic E: Emitter) so a mock can be injected from this test and assert both the editor emit AND its ordering relative to the feed_state_changed emit; this is achievable in Sprint 4 without nightly Tauri features (Emitter is just a trait — define a local TestEmitter that records (event_name, payload) tuples in a Vec and assert order), or (b) extract the inner composition of select_past_note into a separate pure function `select_past_note_compose(vault, note_id) -> (FeedDomainSnapshotDto, Option<EditingSessionStateDto>)` and have the test call THAT, so a regression in the orchestration layer (missing parse, swapped args, missing emit) shows up in this test rather than only in code review. Until either is done, the existing FIND-S4-IMPL-002 finding remains open and the integration_smoke test misrepresents its coverage scope by name.
